title: Quarto Coverage Tracking
text:
1 Documentation Home
This Quarto book documents the coverage-tracking feature described in the repository README.md.
Use the chapters in this book to track implementation decisions, scope, and progress.
crumbs: 1  Documentation Home

title: Quarto Coverage Tracking
text:
1.1 Current chapters

spec.qmd: functional and technical specification for the first implementation.
GUIDE-FOR-CONTENT-EDITORS-AND-TEACHERS.qmd: setup and operations guide for instructors and editors.
crumbs: 1  Documentation Home

title: 2  Coverage Tracking Spec
text:
2.1 Goal
Provide a portable coverage-tracking add-on for Quarto books so students can see what has been covered and professors can compare sections, edit coverage, and maintain data in human-friendly files.
The implementation is static-site friendly (GitHub Pages compatible) and easy to move into a larger yrPublish feature collection.
crumbs: 2  Coverage Tracking Spec

title: 2  Coverage Tracking Spec
text:
2.2 Scope

2.2.1 In scope

Section-level coverage keyed by page URL + HTML section ID.
Student and professor modes in one JavaScript/CSS add-on.
CSV-based coverage data (one file per class section).
Professor UI tools for selecting coverage and exporting CSV text.
Helper scripts for adding missing heading IDs and updating coverage CSVs.



2.2.2 Out of scope

Auth and server-side write APIs.
Hosted database dependency.
Real-time writes from browser directly into repository files.
crumbs: 2  Coverage Tracking Spec

title: 2  Coverage Tracking Spec
text:
2.3 Functional requirements

2.3.1 FR-1: Coverage identity

Coverage is keyed by:

URL (for example /chapter1.html)
HTML ID (for example sec-limits-review)
Class section ID (for example MATH101-01)




2.3.2 FR-2: Student section picker

On first visit, students must choose exactly one class section.
Content is blurred until selection is made.
Student selection is stored per browser tab/session (sessionStorage).
Deep links must still trigger picker gating when no section is selected.



2.3.3 FR-3: Student highlighting

Covered items are highlighted.
Missing coverage entry defaults to not covered.
Sidebar control shows selected section name and lets student reset/re-pick.



2.3.4 FR-4: Professor mode and navigation

Professor mode activates with ?prof or ?prof=true.
Professor chooser supports selecting zero or more sections using checkboxes.
Chooser is not auto-opened on navigation; opens when professor clicks sidebar controls.
Esc dismisses chooser without applying changes.
Internal links preserve prof query parameter.



2.3.5 FR-5: Professor sidebar controls

Sidebar top shows selected professor sections as color-coded entries.
Clicking any selected section entry re-opens chooser.
If none selected, show Choose section(s) entry.
Include Edit page coverage CSV entry to open CSV editor.



2.3.6 FR-6: Coverage data file format

coverage-config.json remains JSON and defines sections and colors.
Coverage data is stored as one CSV file per class section.
CSV row formats:

URL,html-id,covered
URL,html-id,not-covered
URL,html-id (defaults to covered)
URL,html-id, (defaults to covered)

If (URL,html-id) is missing from a section CSV, default is not covered.



2.3.7 FR-7: Professor CSV editing UI

Professor can open page-level coverage editor in the UI.
Editor supports:

select all page IDs as covered
clear all page IDs as not covered
individually toggle each page ID
generate CSV output
copy CSV text
download CSV file

Workflow remains static-first: professor can copy/download and save offline.



2.3.8 FR-8: Helper scripts

tools/yrAddSectionsIds.sh

input: one .qmd file
output: new .qmd with missing heading IDs inserted
skips fenced code blocks so # inside code is ignored

tools/yrUpdateCoverage.sh

input: section coverage CSV + one or more .qmd files
output: new .csv with missing (URL,html-id) rows added
adds missing rows as not-covered
crumbs: 2  Coverage Tracking Spec

title: 2  Coverage Tracking Spec
text:
2.4 Proposed file structure
assets/coverage-tracking/
  coverage-config.json
  coverage-data/
    <SECTION-ID>.csv
  coverage-tracker.js
  coverage-tracker.css
  coverage-tracker.html
tools/
  yrAddSectionsIds.sh
  yrUpdateCoverage.sh
crumbs: 2  Coverage Tracking Spec

title: 2  Coverage Tracking Spec
text:
2.5 CSV semantics
Example section file:
URL,html-id,covered
/index.html,sec-how-to-use,covered
/index.html,sec-reading-plan,not-covered
/chapter1.html,sec-limits-review,
Interpretation:

Third column covered => covered
Third column not-covered => not covered
Missing/blank third column => covered
Missing row entirely => not covered
crumbs: 2  Coverage Tracking Spec

title: 2  Coverage Tracking Spec
text:
2.6 Sample demo book
The sample implementation is in sampleBooks/sample1/ and demonstrates:

student picker and highlighting
professor compare mode
professor sidebar controls
professor CSV editor/export workflow
CSV-per-section coverage loading
crumbs: 2  Coverage Tracking Spec

title: 2  Coverage Tracking Spec
text:
2.7 Risks and mitigations

Missing stable section IDs:

mitigate with yrAddSectionsIds.sh and explicit {#id} authoring practice.

Inconsistent URLs between source and rendered pages:

mitigate with conventions in tooling and editor guide.

Theme collisions:

mitigate with namespaced .ct-* CSS selectors.
crumbs: 2  Coverage Tracking Spec

title: 2  Coverage Tracking Spec
text:
2.8 Definition of done

Coverage runtime reads coverage-config.json + per-section CSV files.
Student mode enforces picker gating and tab-scoped persistence.
Professor mode supports comparison, navigation persistence, sidebar controls, and chooser dismiss-on-Esc.
Professor can generate/export CSV coverage from UI.
Tools scripts exist and are documented.
Docs include setup and operations guide for editors/professors.
crumbs: 2  Coverage Tracking Spec

title: 3  Guide for Content Editors and Teachers
text:
3.1 Who this guide is for
This guide is for people who maintain Quarto books and track class coverage.
Students do not need special documentation to consume the book.
crumbs: 3  Guide for Content Editors and Teachers

title: 3  Guide for Content Editors and Teachers
text:
3.2 What this system does

Students pick a class section and see covered content highlighted.
Professors can compare multiple sections in ?prof mode.
Coverage data is maintained in section-specific CSV files.
Professors can generate updated CSV content from the website UI.
crumbs: 3  Guide for Content Editors and Teachers

title: 3  Guide for Content Editors and Teachers
text:
3.3 Required directory structure
In your Quarto book project, include:
assets/coverage-tracking/
  coverage-config.json
  coverage-data/
    <SECTION-ID>.csv
  coverage-tracker.js
  coverage-tracker.css
  coverage-tracker.html
Optional helper scripts:
tools/
  yrAddSectionsIds.sh
  yrUpdateCoverage.sh
crumbs: 3  Guide for Content Editors and Teachers

title: 3  Guide for Content Editors and Teachers
text:
3.4 File formats

3.4.1 coverage-config.json (JSON, required)
Defines available sections and styling.
{
  "sections": [
    {
      "id": "MATH101-01",
      "label": "MATH101 Section 01",
      "color": "#2e7d32",
      "coverageCsv": "assets/coverage-tracking/coverage-data/MATH101-01.csv"
    }
  ]
}


3.4.2 coverage-data/<SECTION-ID>.csv (CSV, required per section)
Each class section has its own coverage file.
Accepted row formats:

URL,html-id,covered
URL,html-id,not-covered
URL,html-id
URL,html-id,

Default semantics:

Missing row => not covered
Blank/missing third column => covered

Example:
URL,html-id,covered
/chapter1.html,sec-limits-review,covered
/chapter1.html,sec-continuity-basics,not-covered
/chapter1.html,sec-derivatives-preview,
crumbs: 3  Guide for Content Editors and Teachers

title: 3  Guide for Content Editors and Teachers
text:
3.5 Quarto integration steps
In your book _quarto.yml:

Include coverage config and CSV directory as project resources.
Include coverage CSS.
Include the tracker script HTML include.

Example:
project:
  type: book
  resources:
    - assets/coverage-tracking/coverage-config.json
    - assets/coverage-tracking/coverage-data/

format:
  html:
    css:
      - assets/coverage-tracking/coverage-tracker.css
    include-after-body:
      - assets/coverage-tracking/coverage-tracker.html
crumbs: 3  Guide for Content Editors and Teachers

title: 3  Guide for Content Editors and Teachers
text:
3.6 Authoring content safely
Coverage is based on HTML IDs, so section headings should have explicit IDs:
## Limits Review {#sec-limits-review}
This makes coverage stable and portable across edits.
crumbs: 3  Guide for Content Editors and Teachers

title: 3  Guide for Content Editors and Teachers
text:
3.7 Helper tools

3.7.1 yrAddSectionsIds.sh
Purpose:

add missing heading IDs in a .qmd file
skip fenced code blocks

Usage:
./tools/yrAddSectionsIds.sh input.qmd [output.qmd]


3.7.2 yrUpdateCoverage.sh
Purpose:

read one section CSV plus one or more .qmd files
add missing (URL,html-id) rows to a new output CSV
missing rows are added as not-covered

Usage:
./tools/yrUpdateCoverage.sh --coverage assets/coverage-tracking/coverage-data/MATH101-01.csv chapter1.qmd chapter2.qmd
crumbs: 3  Guide for Content Editors and Teachers

title: 3  Guide for Content Editors and Teachers
text:
3.8 Professor workflow in UI

3.8.1 Compare mode

Open book with ?prof or ?prof=true.
Use sidebar entry to open section chooser.
Select zero or more sections with checkboxes.
Apply and review highlighted overlap.



3.8.2 Edit page coverage CSV

In professor mode, click Edit page coverage CSV in sidebar.
Choose the class section to edit.
Use:

Select all on page
Clear all on page
individual checkbox toggles per section ID

Click Build CSV.
Copy or download output and save/update the section CSV offline.
crumbs: 3  Guide for Content Editors and Teachers

title: 3  Guide for Content Editors and Teachers
section: 3.9 Notes on defaults and new content
text:
3.9 Notes on defaults and new content

If a new heading is added but not listed in CSV, it is interpreted as not covered.
This default is useful during course progression because coverage accumulates over time.
crumbs: 3  Guide for Content Editors and Teachers

title: 3  Guide for Content Editors and Teachers
section: 3.10 Moving this feature into another repository
text:
3.10 Moving this feature into another repository
To copy this feature into a larger Quarto feature collection:

Copy assets/coverage-tracking/.
Copy tools/ scripts if desired.
Update target book _quarto.yml.
Render and test student + professor modes.
crumbs: 3  Guide for Content Editors and Teachers
